titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Rufus B. Sage Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 3168414.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday April 18, 2021 at 03:00

did.unittitle: Rufus B. Sage papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

bioghist.head: Rufus B. Sage, 1817-1893
bioghist.p: Rufus B. Sage left his home in Upper Middletown, Connecticut, in 1836 for Marietta, Ohio, where his sister Jerusha Sage Gear, lived. Except for the winter of 1837-38 when he was in West Virginia and made a voyage down the Ohio and Mississippi to Natchez and Fort Gibson, Sage spent most of the period between 1836 and 1840 in Marietta, Circleville, and Columbus, Ohio, where he engaged in the printing and newspaper business. In 1840, he decided to go to the Rocky Mountains and Oregon, and travelled with some friends to Independence, Missouri, to join a fur-trading party. The party left Independence on September 2, 1841, spent the winter in the mountains, returned to Independence, and went west again in 1842. By 1844, Sage was back in Ohio, editing The Whig Battering-Ram from August 9 to October 25 and preparing to write an account of his travels, which was published by Carey & Hart of Philadelphia in 1846 with the following title: Scenes in the Rocky Mountains, and in Oregon, California, New Mexico, Texas, and the Grand Prairies .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Rufus B. Sage Papers include correspondence of Rufus B. Sage's grandfather Elisha Sage, his father Rufus Sage, and other members of the family, concerning the family's personal affairs and life in Connecticut, 1789-1828. In addition, the collection consists of letters and papers of Rufus B. Sage about his life in Ohio, his travels in the West and the publication of his book, Scenes in the Rocky Mountains, and in Oregon, California, New Mexico, Texas, and the Grand Prairies .
One of the original poems in the collection, "Night on the Prairie," is printed in Scenes in the Rocky Mountains .
The collection consists of 108 letters and papers, 268 pages in all.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This finding aid, created before the advent of computer-generated files, has been converted into a ASCII data file by means of scanning and Optical Character Recognition software. While attempts have been made to retain the complete information from the original document a number of format changes have been made to present the structure of this archive in accordance with current practice.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Rufus B. Sage papers collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Rufus B. Sage Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

did.unittitle: Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The family papers include correspondence of Rufus B. Sage's grandfather Elisha Sage, his father Rufus Sage, and other members of the family, about their personal affairs and life in Connecticut, 1789-1828; and the following letters and papers of Rufus B. Sage about his life in Ohio, his travels in the West and the publication of his book.

did.unittitle: [Photocopies of previous organizational scheme]
Genealogical Record
Clippings concerning Rufus B. Sage's Three Years in the Rockies. 11 clippings.
Beardslee, Nezekiah [?]. Letter to Elisha Sage. 1pp.
Butler, Charles W. Poem: "My Mother's Grave". Undated. 1pp.
Carey & Hart. 14 letters to R. B. Sage. 19 pp.
Dowd, Lewis B. Letter to R. B. Sage. 3 pp.
Gear, Mrs. Frances S. Five letters to her brother, R. B. Sage. 11 pp.
Sage, Mrs. Jerusha Butler. Five letters to her son, R. B. Sage. 8 pp.
Gear, Mrs. Jerusha S. Letter to her brother, Elisha L. Sage. 1pp.
Gear, Mrs. Jerusha S. Nine letters to her brother, Rufus B. Sage. 25 pp.
Gear, Mrs. Jerusha S. Three letters to her mother, Mrs. Jerusha B. Sage. 9 pp.

Hepburn, James, Pastor of the Second Baptist Church, Middletown. Certificate that notice of the intended marriage of Mr. Rufus B. Sage and Miss Marietta M. Miller was given publicly before the congregation, November 7, 1847. Upper Middletown. 1 p.
Merwin, Wealthy Sage. Letter to her cousin, R. B. Sage. 3 pp.
Munson [?], Thomas A. Two letters to R. B. Sage. 4 pp.
Nash, Simeon. Letter to R. B. Sage. 2 pp.
Paine & Burgess. Letter to R. B. Sage. 1 p.
Pierce, F. N. Letter to R. B. Sage. 3 pp.
Sage, Alvin. Letter to his brother, Rufus Sage. 1 pp.
Sage, Amos. Letter to his brother, Rufus Sage. 2 pp.
Sage, Edmund. Seven letters to his brother, R. B. Sage. 13 pp.
Sage, Elisha. Three letters to his wife. 4 pp.
Sage, Elizabeth Maria. Three letters to her uncle, R. B. Sage. 9 pp.
Sage, Elizabeth Williams. Letter to her uncle, R. B. Sage. 3 pp.
Sage, Fanny. Letter to Hannah Jones. 1 pp.
Sage, George Hubbard. Two letters to his uncle, R. B. Sage. 11 pp.
Sage, Martha. Letter to her mother, Mrs. Jerusha B. Sage. 2 pp.

descriptivenote.p: SAGE, MARTHA. Letter to her mother, Mrs. Jerusha B. Sage

did.unittitle: Sage, Rufus B. Letter to his brother, Amos Sage. 2 pp.
Sage, Rufus B. Letter to his sister, Mrs. Francis S. Gear. 2 pp.
Sage, Rufus B. Eight letters to his mother, Mrs. Jerusha B. Sage. 30 pp.
Sage, Rufus B. Six letters to his mother, Mrs. Jerusha B. Sage. 26 pp.
Sage, Rufus B. Six letters to his mother, Mrs. Jerusha B. Sage. 17 pp.
Sage, Rufus B. Letter to his sister, Mrs. Jerusha S. Gear. 4 pp.
Sage, Rufus B. Letter to Messrs. Paine & Burgess. 3 pp.
Sage, Rufus B. "Description of the remains of an ancient fortification at Selsertown, eleven miles north of Natchez, Miss." 2 pp. 49 cm.
Sage, Rufus B. Eight poems. 21 pp. 24½ - 42 cm.
Sage, Rufus B. Memorandum of accounts with Carey & Hart for the publication of Scenes in the Rocky Mountains. 6 pp. 12½ cm.
Steward, A. W. Letter to R. B. Sage. 1 p.
White, Henry. Letter to R. B. Sage. 3 pp.
Woodruff, A. S. Two letters to his sister, Mrs. Jerusha B. Sage. 8 pp.
Woodruff, George R. Letter to his cousin, R. B. Sage. 3 pp.
